William S. Downing papers, 1922-2006.
Collection Number: 6971

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
William S. Downing papers, 1922-2006.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
6971
Abstract:
Collection consists of project files, correspondence, meeting notes, architectural drawings, specifications, photographs, photographic negatives, and slides related to Downing's architectural practice.
Creator:
Downing, William S., 1920-2011.
Quanitities:
309 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

William Smith Downing (1920-2011) was an architect and entrepreneur who spent most of his career working in Ithaca, New York. Downing was born in Orange, New Jersey and studied at the University of Virginia, graduating with a degree in architecture in 1946. During his early career, he practiced architecture in Charlottesville, Virginia and in New York City, where he worked for Skidmore, Owings, and Merrill and Edward Stone. After moving to Ithaca in 1959, Downing joined a local architectural firm and then established William Downing Associates. His firm was known for school additions, including Ithaca High School, as well as commercial buildings, including the Monarch Machine Tool Company located in Cortland, New York. In 1971, Downing purchased the former Ithaca junior high and high school, renovating the building into the DeWitt Mall, a successful example of adaptive reuse. The William S. Downing Prize was established at Cornell University in 1986 and is awarded annually in recognition of outstanding achievement in architectural design in the College of Architecture, Art, and Planning.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Collection consists of project files, correspondence, meeting notes, architectural drawings, specifications, photographs, photographic negatives, and slides related to Downing's architectural practice. Also includes architectural drawings made by other Ithaca architects during the early 20th century.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

William S. Downing papers, #6971. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:

Access to Cornell University building plans, specifications, and project files restricted to permission of the office of Planning Design and Construction.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Downing, William S., 1920-2011.
Places:
Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Architecture.
Subjects:
Architecture -- United States -- History -- 21st century.
Architecture -- United States -- History -- 20th century
Form and Genre Terms:
Negatives (photographic).
Photographs
Blueprints.
Architectural drawings.
